amendments.normalized_body: In `unique_terminal_replay_match` (fs.rs:2838-2844), the contract violation guard logs `tracing::error!` and returns `None`, which causes the caller to return a generic `CorruptRecord` error ("no matching task run" or "ambiguous task run update"). Issue 2 requires "runtime checks that return errors" — the current path does return an error, but the error message seen by the caller describes data corruption rather than the actual programmer contract violation (run_id supplied without started_at). Consider returning an explicit `Err` from `unique_terminal_replay_match` (changing the return type to `AppResult<Option<usize>>`) so the contract violation surfaces as its own distinct error rather than being masked by a misleading CorruptRecord wrapper. This prevents future debugging confusion if the contract is ever violated.

`src/adapters/fs.rs:3040-3058` deliberately stops backfilling `plan_hash` when a new `TaskRunEntry` is created, and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2583-2622` only backfills existing rows when `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` matches the current snapshot, skipping every `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation == None` row. That leaves Issue 3 unimplemented: callers that omit `plan_hash` still produce `TaskRunEntry { plan_hash: None }` for new starts and for many existing/open rows, even though the accepted contract requires adapter-level snapshot backfill in both cases. The updated tests in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:4727-4817`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5274-5285`, and `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5635-5844` now assert the opposite behavior, so the regression is masked instead of covered.

In `src/adapters/fs.rs:3118-3176`, the `run_id` update path returns an error as soon as it sees a single open runless row whose `started_at` differs from the request. That means a replay of an older completed runless attempt is still rejected once a newer retry has started: the code never falls through to the terminal replay matcher at `src/adapters/fs.rs:3127-3152`, even if a completed row exists with the requested `started_at`. The accepted Issue 2 fix requires started-at-based disambiguation for terminal replays too; this branch still breaks the partial-write repair path described in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:527-530`.

final_accepted_amendments.normalized_body: In `unique_terminal_replay_match` (fs.rs:2838-2844), the contract violation guard logs `tracing::error!` and returns `None`, which causes the caller to return a generic `CorruptRecord` error ("no matching task run" or "ambiguous task run update"). Issue 2 requires "runtime checks that return errors" — the current path does return an error, but the error message seen by the caller describes data corruption rather than the actual programmer contract violation (run_id supplied without started_at). Consider returning an explicit `Err` from `unique_terminal_replay_match` (changing the return type to `AppResult<Option<usize>>`) so the contract violation surfaces as its own distinct error rather than being masked by a misleading CorruptRecord wrapper. This prevents future debugging confusion if the contract is ever violated.

In `src/adapters/fs.rs:3118-3176`, the `run_id` update path returns an error as soon as it sees a single open runless row whose `started_at` differs from the request. That means a replay of an older completed runless attempt is still rejected once a newer retry has started: the code never falls through to the terminal replay matcher at `src/adapters/fs.rs:3127-3152`, even if a completed row exists with the requested `started_at`. The accepted Issue 2 fix requires started-at-based disambiguation for terminal replays too; this branch still breaks the partial-write repair path described in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:527-530`.

payload.summary: Final review accepted 2 amendment(s); restart required.
